skill.name: huawei-cloud-modelarts-skill
skill.description: Manage Huawei Cloud ModelArts resources, training jobs, models, inference services, and notebooks using runtime credentials.
skill.summary: Operate Huawei Cloud ModelArts resources from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
skill.author: modelarts-agent

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography, README command snippets, hardcoded URLs, and entropy are mostly false positives after context review. The confirmed risk is operational: the skill uses runtime Huawei Cloud credentials to list cloud resources and create ModelArts training jobs, which can expose metadata or incur cost if permissions are too broad.

medium_findings.title: Runtime Cloud Resource Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill obtains a ModelArts Session and uses it to list OBS buckets, jobs, models, services, and notebooks. It can also create a training job from caller-provided parameters, which may access cloud resources or incur cost.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly uses Huawei ModelArts SDK classes with a runtime Session. The behavior appears legitimate for the skill purpose, but it has real cloud-side effects.

medium_findings.title: Pattern-Based Secret Redaction
medium_findings.description: The redaction helper masks selected key names and strings that contain sensitive words. It may miss secrets under other field names, and one resource overview path returns the raw exception string.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowlist of sensitive field names is visible and limited. This is a moderate concern because returned SDK objects or exceptions may include unexpected fields.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Recommends Public Read Access
medium_findings.description: The README suggests configuring an OBS bucket for public read access as a troubleshooting option. This can expose datasets or model artifacts if users apply it broadly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recommendation is explicit in the troubleshooting text. It is documentation risk rather than executable malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: README Command Findings Are Documentation Snippets
low_findings.description: The external command alerts in README.md point to repository setup and contribution examples such as git and pip commands. These are not executed by the skill at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are inside Markdown documentation blocks. No code path in the reviewed runtime files executes these README strings.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts refer to GitHub, Huawei Cloud console, Huawei support documentation, and a PyPI mirror. No evidence found that these links exfiltrate data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links or package index examples. They are not called from executable skill code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts appear to match names such as desensitize_data, ModelArts metadata, or redaction helper calls. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain imports, descriptions, SDK outputs, and recursive redaction calls. They do not implement or invoke cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: Dangerous Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed as Malicious
low_findings.description: The scanner combined documentation commands, public URLs, and credential-related redaction names into a critical heuristic. Manual review found no evidence of prompt injection, hidden exfiltration, or unauthorized command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The individual signals are present, but their context is benign or operational. The remaining risk comes from intended cloud API use, not confirmed malicious composition.

dangerous_patterns.title: Resource-Creating Action Uses Caller Parameters
dangerous_patterns.description: The create_training_job action accepts code directory, boot file, job name, and flavor from the request and submits a ModelArts training job. Users should restrict permissions and validate allowed inputs before production use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The SDK call that creates a job is visible and uses caller-provided fields. This is expected functionality, but it is the most important operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Raw Exception Message on Resource Overview Failure
dangerous_patterns.description: The resource overview function returns str(e) on failure. Provider exceptions can sometimes include account, endpoint, or permission details.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The raw exception return is direct evidence. The exact sensitivity depends on ModelArts and OBS SDK exception content.

content.value_statement: ModelArts users need a faster way to inspect AI training and deployment resources. This skill provides guided actions for resource overview, jobs, models, services, and notebooks.
actual_capabilities: Lists a basic resource overview using the active ModelArts session and OBS bucket count.
Lists ModelArts training jobs with a caller-selected result limit.
Creates a ModelArts training job from a code directory, boot file, job name, and flavor.
Lists registered models, inference services, and notebooks through the ModelArts SDK.
Masks common credential, token, endpoint, VPC, subnet, and security group fields in returned data.
Routes requests through a fixed action map for the supported ModelArts operations.
limitations: Requires valid Huawei Cloud ModelArts runtime credentials in the execution environment.
Does not include actions to delete, stop, restart, or update cloud resources.
Uses pattern-based redaction, so unusual secret field names may not be masked.
Most operation errors are generic, which can make troubleshooting harder.

use_cases.title: Check Resource State
use_cases.description: Review ModelArts jobs, services, notebooks, models, and OBS resource counts before a deployment window.

use_cases.title: Launch Training Work
use_cases.description: Submit a training job from prepared ModelArts code and choose the required compute flavor.

use_cases.title: Audit Active Assets
use_cases.description: Collect a quick inventory of ModelArts resources while masking common credential and network identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: View Resource Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Huawei Cloud ModelArts skill to show my current resource overview. Summarize the OBS bucket count and any returned status message.

prompt_templates.title: List Training Jobs
prompt_templates.prompt: List my recent ModelArts training jobs with a limit of 10. Highlight job names, states, and any masked fields in the response.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Deployment Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: List my ModelArts models, inference services, and notebooks. Group the results by resource type and flag anything that looks inactive.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Training Job
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced job submission with explicit parameters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ModelArts training job using my approved code directory, boot file, job name, and compute flavor. Confirm the submitted job status and masked output.

output_examples.output: The skill reports a successful overview request and returns the OBS bucket count. Sensitive account details are not shown.

output_examples.input: List my latest training jobs with a limit of five.
output_examples.output: The response contains a concise list of recent training jobs. Credential-like fields are masked before display.

output_examples.input: Create a training job from my prepared code package.
output_examples.output: The skill submits the job through ModelArts and returns the submitted job information with sensitive fields removed.

best_practices: Run the skill with least-privilege ModelArts credentials that match the intended action set.
Review job parameters before creating training jobs, because cloud resources and cost may be affected.
Keep OBS buckets private unless a specific workflow requires a narrow public access rule.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill with broad administrator credentials for routine inventory tasks.
Do not paste long-lived AK/SK values into prompts or logs.
Do not rely on pattern-based masking as the only protection for sensitive cloud metadata.

faq.question: What cloud service does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Huawei Cloud ModelArts operations for resources, training jobs, models, inference services, and notebooks.

faq.question: Does it store credentials?
faq.answer: No storage is implemented in the reviewed files. It creates a ModelArts Session from runtime credentials.

faq.question: Can it create cloud resources?
faq.answer: Yes. It can submit a ModelArts training job, so permissions and parameters should be reviewed first.

faq.question: Does it delete ModelArts resources?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed action map does not include delete, stop, restart, or update actions.

faq.question: Are returned secrets masked?
faq.answer: Common credential, token, endpoint, subnet, VPC, and security group fields are masked by name or keyword.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is suitable for publication with a medium-risk warning about cloud permissions, job creation, and pattern-based redaction.
